chunks.en: Today I want to talk about asking for help.
chunks.ja: 今日は助けを求めることについて話したいです。

chunks.en: When I need help, I go and ask someone near me.
chunks.ja: 困ったとき、近くの人に聞きに行きます。

chunks.en: I usually ask a person who knows the work well.
chunks.ja: たいてい仕事をよく知っている人に頼みます。

chunks.en: I try to say what I need in a short way.
chunks.ja: 必要なことを短く伝えるようにします。

chunks.en: For example, I ask about a bug in my code.
chunks.ja: たとえば、自分のコードのバグについて聞きます。

chunks.en: After they help me, I always say thank you.
chunks.ja: 助けてもらったら必ずお礼を言います。

chunks.en: Asking for help makes my work easier and faster.
chunks.ja: 助けを求めると仕事が楽で速くなります。

chunks.en: So don't be afraid to ask your coworkers.
chunks.ja: だから同僚に聞くことを怖がらないでください。

outline: Opening: talk about asking for help
Point 1: go ask someone who knows the work
Point 2: say your need clearly, then say thank you
Wrap-up: asking makes work easier, so don't be afraid

modelTalk.text: Sometimes I get stuck at work. When I need help, I go and ask someone near me. I don't wait too long. I usually ask a person who knows the work well. They can help me fast. I try to say what I need in a short way. I keep it clear and simple. I don't talk too much. I just tell them the problem. Then I listen and I take notes. After they help me, I always say thank you. It's polite, and it feels good. I'm happy to learn something new. Next time, maybe I can help them too. That's how we work well together.

chunks.en: Today I want to talk about asking a coworker for help.
chunks.ja: 今日は同僚に助けを求めることについて話したいです。

chunks.en: When I need help, I go and ask someone near me.
chunks.ja: 困ったとき、私は近くの人に聞きに行きます。

chunks.en: I usually ask a person who knows the work well.
chunks.ja: たいてい仕事をよく知っている人に頼みます。

chunks.en: I try to say what I need in a short way.
chunks.ja: 必要なことを短く伝えるようにしています。

chunks.en: For example, I ask about a bug in my code.
chunks.ja: 例えば、コードのバグについて聞きます。

chunks.en: After they help me, I always say thank you.
chunks.ja: 助けてもらったら、必ずお礼を言います。

chunks.en: So asking for help makes my work much easier.
chunks.ja: だから助けを求めることで、仕事がずっと楽になります。

outline: Opening: asking a coworker for help
Point 1: I ask someone near me who knows the work
Point 2: I say my need clearly, like a code bug
Wrap-up: I say thanks and work gets easier

modelTalk.text: Sometimes I need help at work. When that happens, I go to someone near me. I don't wait too long. I usually ask a person who knows the work well. That person can help me fast. I try to say what I need. I keep it short and clear. I don't talk for too long. So the other person understands me quickly. When they help me, I say thank you. I always do this. It's kind and it feels good. Asking for help isn't a bad thing. We all need help sometimes. So I ask, and I'm thankful. That's how I work with my coworkers.
